title: Cells, Walls, Passages, Boundaries, and Invariants · Program Brief: Model View Separation
summary: One cell opens east while its neighbor stays closed west, boundary walls disappear, and rendering data becomes the maze source of truth. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the model view separation increment in reciprocal wall and passage model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Keep maze topology independent from Canvas item identifiers, colors, widths, and animation state.
Preserve outer-boundary rules while supporting explicit entrance and exit exceptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-model-view-separation — Keep maze topology independent from Canvas item identifiers, colors, widths, and animation state.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-wall-cell-invariants-4
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-wall-cell-invariants-4.
# Case 1655: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def worked_maze_wall_cell_invariants_4(cells: int = 48, reciprocal_openings: int = 47, passage_edges: int = 47, boundary_breaches: int = 0, illegal_diagonals: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "gridworked4",
        "topology1655",
        "search4dekrow",
        "eventworked41655",
        "access1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or passage_edges < 0:
        return False, passage_edges, "topology-count"
    if reciprocal_openings != passage_edges:
        return False, reciprocal_openings, "reciprocal-wall"
    if boundary_breaches > 0:
        return False, boundary_breaches, "boundary-policy"
    if illegal_diagonals > 0:
        return False, illegal_diagonals, "illegal-neighbor"
    assert reciprocal_openings == passage_edges
    return True, passage_edges, "valid-wall-model"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-model-view-separation-2t0v54
# Competency: maze-model-view-separation.
# Case 9500: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def evidence_maze_model_view_separation_2t0v54(cells: int = 48, reciprocal_openings: int = 47, passage_edges: int = 47, boundary_breaches: int = 0, illegal_diagonals: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_2t0v54 = "2t0v54-45v0t2-9500"
    assert evidence_variant_2t0v54
    evidence_axes_2t0v54 = (
        "grid2t0v54",
        "topology9500",
        "search45v0t2",
        "event2t0v549500",
        "access95002t0v54",
        "recovery45v0t29500",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_2t0v54)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or passage_edges < 0:
        return False, passage_edges, "topology-count"
    if reciprocal_openings != passage_edges:
        return False, reciprocal_openings, "reciprocal-wall"
    if boundary_breaches > 0:
        return False, boundary_breaches, "boundary-policy"
    if illegal_diagonals > 0:
        return False, illegal_diagonals, "illegal-neighbor"
    assert reciprocal_openings == passage_edges
    return True, passage_edges, "valid-wall-model"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Keep maze topology independent from Canvas item identifiers, colors, widths, and animation state.
